exercises.title: A: Extra word
exercises.instructions: If a line is correct, put a tick (✓). If there is an extra word in a line, write the word.
exercises.studentInstruction: If the line is correct, tap OK. If there is an extra word, tap it.

items.sentence: Barcelona is famous for its beaches and beautiful buildings.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "✓" is redundant here and should be removed.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Read the sentence aloud without the tapped word — if it sounds natural, that word is the extra one.

wrongOptions.Barcelona: "Barcelona" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.is: "is" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.famous: "famous" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.for: "for" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.its: "its" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.beaches: "beaches" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.and: "and" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.beautiful: "beautiful" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.buildings: "buildings" is necessary here — the extra word is "✓".
wrongOptions.OK: There is an extra word ("✓") — do not tap OK.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Look for a repeated or unnecessary small word — "✓" is the one to tap, not a content word the sentence needs.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Try reading the sentence without each word in your head — the extra word is the one that can be dropped with no loss of meaning.

exercises.title: B: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Did the hotel give you clean towels? **provide**
Did the hotel ...... clean towels?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Provide you with = give you (same meaning).
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
My plan is to visit Greece next summer. **going**
I ...... visit Greece next summer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is a plan, so the correct answer is "am going to".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "am going to", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
We prefer hotels by the sea. **coast**
We prefer hotels ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is hotels by the sea, so the correct answer is "on the coast".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "on the coast", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
Be careful when you leave the train. **off**
Be careful when you ...... the train.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is leave the train, so the correct answer is "get off".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "get off", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
The museum is near, so we can walk there. **foot**
We can ...... to the museum because it's near.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is walk there, so the correct answer is "go on foot".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "go on foot", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence:
Why don't you travel to Oxford in your car? **car**
Why don't you go to Oxford ...... ?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is travel in your car, so the correct answer is "by car".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "by car", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
We're going to return to Rome next year. **back**
We're going to ...... Rome next year.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is return, so the correct answer is "go back to".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "go back to", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
I enjoy watching planes leave the ground. **off**
I enjoy watching planes ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is planes leave the ground, so the correct answer is "taking off".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "taking off", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

exercises.title: C: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: We wanted to take the train, but in the end we ...... . (FLY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past simple of fly, so the correct answer is "flew".

wrongOptions.fly: The root FLY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "flew".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of FLY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: FLY → flew: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "flew" is the word-formation answer from the root FLY in this context.

items.sentence: The taxi ...... helped us put our luggage in the car. (DRIVE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is person who drives, so the correct answer is "driver".

wrongOptions.drive: The root DRIVE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "driver".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DRIVE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: DRIVE → driver: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "driver" is the word-formation answer from the root DRIVE in this context.

wrongOptions.travel: The root TRAVEL cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "travellers".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of TRAVEL that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: TRAVEL → travellers: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "travellers" is the word-formation answer from the root TRAVEL in this context.

wrongOptions.attract: The root ATTRACT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "attractive".
